title: Protocol to Process Gastruloids for FACS
author: Stefano D Vianello
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvgrn3v6

steps.title: Once all Gastruloids have settled to...
steps.instruction: Once all Gastruloids have settled to the bottom of the tube, aspirate out the supernatant (N2B27 medium carried over with each Gastruloids)

steps.title: Resuspend the Gastruloids in 5mL PBS-/-,...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the Gastruloids in 5mL PBS-/-, to wash away traces of N2B27

steps.title: Once all Gastruloids have re-settled to...
steps.instruction: Once all Gastruloids have re-settled to the bottom of the tube, aspirate out the PBS-/-. Gastruloids are ready to be digested.

steps.title: Digest the Gastruloids by adding 1mL...
steps.instruction: Digest the Gastruloids by adding 1mL Digestion Solution (3mg/mL Collagenase IV, 4mg/mL Dispase, 100ug/mL DNAseI, in PBS-/-), 37 °C, (waterbath) , 00:04:00 min

steps.title: After the 4min incubation has elapsed,...
steps.instruction: After the 4min incubation has elapsed, use a P1000 coated in Staining Buffer to try to mechanically disrupt the Gastruloids

steps.title: Place the tube back again at...
steps.instruction: Place the tube back again at 37 °C, (waterbath) , 00:04:00 min; to complete the digestion

steps.title: After the last 4min of incubation...
steps.instruction: After the last 4min of incubation have elapsed, use a P1000 coated in Staining Buffer (see recipe in "Materials" section) to mechanically disrupt the Gastruloids. Gastruloids should break easily and give rise to a single cell suspension.

steps.title: Prewet the blue cap of a filter-FACS tube (e
steps.instruction: Prewet the blue cap of a filter-FACS tube (e.g. CAT#352235, Falcon/Corning), by pipetting 1mL of Staining Buffer through it. This step is very imprtant. Not prewetting the filter can lead to loss of a high number of cells at the filtering step.

steps.title: Working On ice Filter your cell...
steps.instruction: Working On ice Filter your cell suspension (digested Gastruloids) through the cap of the FACS tube

steps.title: On ice Wash the filter by...
steps.instruction: On ice Wash the filter by passing 1mL more of DIgestion Solution through it.

steps.title: On ice Transfer the contents of...
steps.instruction: On ice Transfer the contents of the FACS tube to a clean 15mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: On ice Collect any leftover cells...
steps.instruction: On ice Collect any leftover cells by flushing the old FACS tube with 2mL Staining Buffer, and transferring in the 15mL Falcon tube with the rest of the cells

steps.title: On ice Add 5mL more Staining...
steps.instruction: On ice Add 5mL more Staining buffer into the tube Falcon tube with your cells suspension, to completely stop the digestion reaction. This tube now contains a filtered, single-cell suspension of your sample for FACS.

steps.title: Spin down the cell suspension, 200...
steps.instruction: Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min

steps.title: For each sample, prepare 500uL of 0
steps.instruction: For each sample, prepare 500uL of 0.2ug/mL DAPI in Staining Buffer From a stock solution of DAPI 1mg/mL, use 1:5000. E.g. add 0.1uL DAPI in 500uL Staining Buffer

steps.title: On ice Resuspend the cell pellet...
steps.instruction: On ice Resuspend the cell pellet in the DAPI solution, and incubate 00:15:00 min, On ice

steps.title: Spin down the cell suspension, 200...
steps.instruction: Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min

steps.title: FIxation
steps.instruction: FIxation: On ice Resuspend the DAPI-stained cell pellet in 2mL 1%PFA,

steps.title: Spin down the cell suspension, 200...
steps.instruction: Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min

steps.title: Resuspend in 300uL Staining Buffer and...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 300uL Staining Buffer and transfer to a clean FACS tube pre-coated with Staining Buffer

steps.title: For samples that come from 2D culture (e
steps.instruction: For samples that come from 2D culture (e.g. 2D-grown reporter cells as negative controls, or 2D-grown single-color cells as positive controls): aspirate out the medium from the culture dish add 3mL PBS-/- to wash away any trace of medium, and aspirate it out again Add 500uL Accutase, to detach the cells, 00:05:00 min, Room temperature Transfer the detached cells +Accutase to a clean Falcon tube Add ~5mL 10% Serum to stop the reaction Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min The cells are ready to be processed just as any Gastruloid sample (proceed to the next step)

steps.title: Harvest Gastruloid from each well of...
steps.instruction: Harvest Gastruloid from each well of a 96 well plate and collect them in a 15mL Falcon tube (pooled by timepoint or condition)

steps.title: Cells can be kept in Staining...
steps.instruction: Cells can be kept in Staining Buffer, 4 °C , in the dark (wrapped in alluminium foil) for at least a week (e.g. while waiting to collect samples from later on timepoints)

steps.title: When ready for FACS, On ice...
steps.instruction: When ready for FACS, On ice ,distribute your cells in 300uL per FACS tube and proceed for sorting/analysis.

steps.title: If using beads as positive references,...
steps.instruction: If using beads as positive references, prepare bead tubes just before FACS processing. For GFP BrightComp eBeads™ Compensation Bead Kit (Invitrogen/Thermo Fisher Scientific, CAT#A10514): vortex vial for 00:00:10 s Dispense 1 drop into a clean FACS tube Resuspend in 1mL Staining Buffer
